unit.scope: Full server vitest suite (101 files, was 100 on origin/main). Includes the new ws-upgrade-handler.test.ts (29 tests covering lifecycle, parse table, atomic detach) and the relocated terminal-reset.test.ts (3 tests, import path moved to ./terminal-reset.js).

e2e.scope: Playwright E2E NOT re-run inside this iterate. The change is a pure server-side WS-handler extraction; the F0.5 Node-side empirical probe (below) drives the live HTTP+WS surface end-to-end against an isolated stack, which is the chokepoint for this refactor. Existing Playwright specs (82-v0.8.6-terminal-reattach-smoke, C5-embedded-terminal-split-smoke) cover the browser-level contract via the same WS envelope shape locked in by the key-parity unit assertion.

smoke.scope: Isolated server boot on PORT=3848 + USERPROFILE=$(mktemp) + SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local. /api/health=200, POST /api/external/tasks=200, WS upgrade succeeded, ws.send/close clean. Server logs show no errors during the probe lifecycle.

surface_verification.notes: Probe 1 (happy path): WS upgrade emits ready envelope with EXACT 10-key set (no drift), all field types correct, ws.send accepted, close(1000) clean. Probe 2 (validation timing): evil origin + unknown task BOTH reject synchronously (HTTP 500, no WS open) - empirically confirms external plan review HIGH #1 contract that validations stay in routes.ts and never degrade to silent WS disconnects.
